compiled_state.title: [record-test-status]: test passes (exit 0) when run directly but records 'failed' in centrality-aware runner -> commit blocked spuriously
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
record-test-status.sh recorded test-ticket-subprocess-count.sh as 'failed' during sprint 2c28-e5b9, blocking a commit. When the test was run directly (bash tests/scripts/test-ticket-subprocess-count.sh), it passed with exit 0 (9 passed, 0 failed). The pre-commit test gate then allowed the commit through, suggesting the gate used a different evaluation path than the initial record-test-status.sh run.

## Impact
Spurious 'failed' recordings cause the pre-commit test gate to block commits that should pass. Developers must investigate why a test that passes interactively was recorded as failed, adding debugging overhead.

## Observed Behavior
- DSO_COMMIT_WORKFLOW=1 bash .claude/scripts/dso record-test-status.sh --source-file tests/scripts/test-ticket-next-batch-dispatcher.sh
- Output: 'Test status recorded: failed — failed tests: tests/scripts/test-ticket-subprocess-count.sh'
- Direct run: bash tests/scripts/test-ticket-subprocess-count.sh → exit 0, PASSED: 9 FAILED: 0

## Root Cause (Unknown)
The discrepancy between interactive run and record-test-status.sh execution suggests an environment difference: working directory, PATH, or environment variables differ when record-test-status.sh spawns the test runner vs. direct invocation. The DeprecationWarning output from Python (datetime.datetime.utcnow()) was present in both runs and is not the cause.

## Suggested Investigation
Add debug logging to record-test-status.sh's test runner invocation to capture the exact command, working directory, and environment variables used. Compare against a direct invocation.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 4 (INTERMEDIATE). Severity=1 (spurious failure blocks commits), Complexity=2 (environment difference), Environment=0, Intermittent=1 (passes interactively, fails in record-test-status runner).
comments.author: Worktree
